error.signature: AI claims opening a US bank account only needs a passport, ignoring the SSN/ITIN requirement for most national banks

verdict.summary: US Patriot Act §326 (Customer Identification Program) requires banks to collect a taxpayer identification number. In practice: SSN for residents, ITIN for non-resident taxpayers. Some banks (Chase, BofA, HSBC, Citi) have foreign-national programs that accept a passport + secondary ID without an SSN, but these accounts are limited and require in-branch application.

dead_ends.action: Tell a newly-arrived foreigner to open a Chase/BofA account fully online with just a passport
dead_ends.why_fails: Online account opening flows require an SSN input field and will reject applications without one. Patriot Act §326 mandates a TIN for every customer; banks refuse to enrol via the digital channel without it.
dead_ends.condition: No SSN/ITIN yet, trying online enrolment

dead_ends.action: Apply for an SSN before getting a US job offer or qualifying visa status
dead_ends.why_fails: SSA only issues SSNs to people authorized to work (F-1 with CPT/OPT, H-1B, green card, etc.). Tourists on B-1/B-2 are categorically ineligible; the correct path for tax-only purposes is ITIN via IRS form W-7.
dead_ends.condition: Need a tax number for banking but not work-authorized

workarounds.action: Apply in-branch at Chase, HSBC, or BofA's International programs with passport + second ID + US address — they enrol foreign nationals without an SSN under a W-8BEN.
workarounds.condition: Have a US address and in-person availability
workarounds.tradeoff: Account features limited until SSN/ITIN is obtained; no credit cards at account opening.

workarounds.action: If you will file a US tax return, submit IRS form W-7 with supporting documents (usually notarized passport copy) to obtain an ITIN, then complete the bank enrolment.
workarounds.condition: Tax filing obligation (interest income, rental, business)

workarounds.action: For pure transactional needs, use fintechs like Wise or Revolut USD accounts that accept a passport without an SSN, then migrate once tax ID is issued.
workarounds.tradeoff: Not a US-domiciled bank account; limited ACH and no personal check issuance.
